behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client handoff (request builder -> API adapter). A pure unit test of a helper can miss wiring bugs where labels are dropped before send. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a stub API client that records outbound payload; invoke classify with a taxonomy and assert every taxonomy label is present in the dispatched request.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about end-to-end handling of provider response through parsing/validation/output boundary, so cross-module wiring can fail even if local mapping logic is correct. Higher levels are disqualified because this does not require real infra or real model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API response containing an out-of-taxonomy label; run classify through the module public entrypoint and assert returned label is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic local normalization logic on fixed input; no module handoff, infra, or model behavior is required to reproduce failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with identical raw response fixtures (case/whitespace variants already fixed in fixture) and assert identical normalized label every run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on live AI provider outputs across repeated calls; mocks would hide real model variability and cannot validate consistency of actual model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real API classifications for fixed document+taxonomy, capture raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label changes only when raw provider output changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Needs interaction between token counting/policy logic and dispatch gate (whether/what gets sent). A single-function unit test can miss boundary bugs where enforcement is bypassed before client call. No real OS/network required.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, send oversized document, and assert module either truncates or rejects per policy and that outbound call payload/dispatch behavior matches policy via stub client.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real AI API call and real provider token-usage metadata; mocks can always fit budget and hide provider-side tokenization/usage behavior. Rejected Workflow: only single model-call step, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents, read provider-reported usage fields, and assert reported prompt/total tokens are <= configured budget (or policy-compliant if provider reports separately).

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across modules (error handling, backoff, re-dispatch). A stub provider emitting rate-limit then success reproduces the real failure boundary; real network/LLM output is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API client to return rate-limit error once (optionally with retry-after) then success; assert module waits/backs off per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing initial error.
